MWCapture SDK  3.3.1.1556
  • Home
  • Development Library
  • Examples
  • Release Notes
NDISender C++

The features are as follows:

  1. specifying whether to send NDI streams.
  2. capturing and previewing video using interfaces of Pro Capture Family Functions.
  3. capturing and previewing audio using interfaces of Pro/Eco Capture Family Universal Functions.
  4. specifying resolution, color space and frame rate.

Folder dir: SDKv3\Examples\VC++\GUI\NDISender

Supported Hardware

Pro Capture Family

Features

  1. Preview captured videos and audios.
  2. Customize resolution, color format and frame rate of captured videos.
  3. Send/Stop sending NDI streams.
  4. Switch capture channel mid-stream. In Addition, it cannot switch capture channel while sending NDI streams.

Workflow

The main thread workflow:
  1. To initialize and enumerate all available devices, call: MWCaptureInitInstance, MWGetChannelCount
  2. To find the valid capture cards and add them into the drop-off menu, call: MWGetChannelInfoByIndex, MWGetDevicePath
  3. To create renders for videos and audios, call: MWDXRender,CDSoundRenderer
  4. To open the default channel, call: MWGetDevicePath, MWOpenChannelByPath
  5. Create a video process thread.
  6. To start an audio capture, call: MWStartAudioCapture
  7. To get supported input audio type and status, call: MWGetAudioInputSourceArray, MWGetAudioSignalStatus
  8. Create an audio process thread.
  9. Enter the main thread.
  10. Customize capture format which cannot be modified during streaming:
    • To close preview, call: MWStopVideoCapture, MWStopAudioCapture;
    • To re-open captured channel, repeat step 4 to 9.
  11. Start sending NDI stream by clicking the "start send" button:
    • Initialize NDI.
    • Create an NDI sender.
    • Establish a send connection;
    • Customize NDI sending parameters.
    • Establish the connection for NDI sending.
  12. To free up system resources and memory after exiting the capture application, call: MWCloseChannel and MWCaptureExitInstance
Video process workflow:
  1. To start a video capture, call: MWStartVideoCapture
  2. To register a timer, call: MWRegisterTimer
  3. To capture a frame of video data triggered by the set timer, call: MWScheduleTimer, MWCaptureVideoFrameToVirtualAddressEx
  4. To get current capture status, and clear the memory of capture card, call: MWGetVideoCaptureStatus
  5. Render captured video.
  6. Repeat step 3 to 5, until the thread quits.
  7. Quit the thread and clear resources.
Audio process workflow
  1. To register notification for a frame cashed and audio signal changed, call: MWRegisterNotify
  2. To capture an audio frame triggered by notification, call: MWGetNotifyStatus, MWCaptureAudioFrame
  3. To render captured audio, call: OnAudioCallback
  4. To get the number and status of the input audio source, then close and re-open the preview triggered by a signal change, call: MWGetAudioInputSourceArray, MWGetAudioSignalStatus, OnAudioSignalChanged
  5. Repeat step 2 to 4, until the thread quits.
  6. To quit thread and free resources, call: MWUnregisterNotify, MWStopAudioCapture

Flow Chart

NDISender

Result

NDISender
